rulings.Case Title: CLARCEEN CALDWELL VS. WALTER SCOTT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Special Interoggs
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Monday, September 15, 2014, Line 16, PLAINTIFFS CLARCEEN CALDWELL, LEON BOOKER, LEJEUNE CARTER, RICHARD PERKINS' Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interoggatories Plaintiffs' Motion to Compel Further Responses to Special Interrogatories Nos. 1 & 4 is denied. Defendants have provided all of the information required under CCP Section 2017.210. The fact that the amount of the remaining limits of coverage may be declining, although possibly relevant to settlement, does not make it admissible or likely to lead to discoverable evidence. Further, defendants have indicated a willingness to provide this requested information in continuation of settlement discussions. No monetary sanctions will be assessed against either party. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: MARK EMMETT WILSON VS. MIGUEL ALEJANDRO RESCALVO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For Judgmnt On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Monday, September 15, 2014, Line 9, CROSS DEFENDANT MARK WILSON'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings The motion for judgment on the pleadings is granted with 20 days leave to amend. Defendant has leave to amend to clarify that it is pleading a cause of action for intentional interference with prospective economic advantage and not an interference with contract claim. Defendant also has leave to plead ultimate facts showing how plaintiff disrupted a relationship with one of the third parties referenced in paragraphs 9-17 of the cross-complaint. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: ELIZABETH SHWIFF et al VS. HARTFORD CASUALTY INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Discretionary Discovery
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, September 15, 2014, Line 2, PLAINTIFF EIZABETH SHWIFF, SHWIFF, LEVY & POLO, LLP Motion For Discretionary Discovery. Pro Tem Judge Peter Catalanotti, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Judge Pro Tem issued the following tentative ruling: The Motion is DENIED without prejudice. The Court recognizes that "[i]f the plaintiff makes a timely and proper showing in response to the motion to strike, that a defendant or witness possesses evidence needed by plaintiff to establish a prima facie case, the plaintiff must be given the reasonable opportunity to obtain that evidence through discovery before the motion to strike is adjudicated." Sipple v. Found. For Nat. Progress (1999) 71 Cal. App. 4th 226, 253. However, if discovery is allowed, it will be limited to the issues raised by the anti-SLAPP motion; i.e., matters that may help plaintiff establish a "reasonable probability of prevailing" on the claim, rather than merely matters to impeach defendant's credibility. Id. at 247. Here, plaintiffs have not made a proper showing that the requested broad discovery ("any and all documents contained within or related to [Hartford's] claims file") is needed by plaintiffs to establish a reasonable probability of prevailing on any cause of action. Plaintiffs fail to identify, any element of any cause of action for which they seek evidence. Further, plaintiffs fail to identify which documents in Hartford's claims file would support the element of the cause of action. Defendants' request to advance the hearing date is DENIED. Defendants failed to allege any good cause. Defendant shall prepare a proposed order and submit the order to the Court by email: pcc@manningllp.com Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to pcc@manningllp.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: 20 PARKRIDGE TIC VS. 20 PARKRIDGE LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition And Petition To Compel Arbitration And To Stay Action Pending Such Arbitration (Ccp 1281.4)
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Monday, September 15, 2014, Line 15, DEFENDANTS 20 PARKRIDGE LLC, LHJS INVESTMENTS LLC, MAGNATE FUND #2, JOHN SIMONSE'S Notice Of Hearing On Petition And Petition To Compel Arbitration And To Stay Action Pending Such Arbitration (CCP 1281.4) The Petition to Compel Arbitration and to Stay Action pending such arbitration is granted. Plaintiff's First Amended Complaint asserts claims for breach of the Purchase Agreement ("PA") (7th cause of action); breach of the Tenancy in Common Agreement ("TICA") (8th and 9th causes of action); breach of contracts between tenants in common and Defendants as to which Plaintiff asserts it is a third party beneficiary (6th cause of action); and claims that are inextricably intertwined with these agreements. Plaintiff seeks damages on behalf of individual owners/tenants in common. The PA and the TICA both contain broad arbitration clauses that encompass Plaintiff's claims. "A nonsignatory plaintiff can be compelled to arbitrate a claim even against a nonsignatory defendant, when the claim is itself based on, or inextricably intertwined with the contract containing the arbitration clause." JSM Tuscany, LLC v. Superior Court, 193 Cal. App. 4th 122, 1241 (2011). Defendant Lehane has not been served and is not a moving defendant on this petition. The Petition to Compel Arbitration is granted as to the moving defendants. The motion to stay action pending the conclusion of the arbitration is granted as to the moving defendants and any claims against Lehane. (See CCP 1281.4.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM
